headlines.description: — Desmond Ridder is still searching for his first touchdown pass three games into his career.
headlines.shortLinkText: Koo kicks short field goal, Falcons edge Arizona 20-19

headlines.description: — Jared Goff has played nearly mistake-free football for two months and his decision-making, along with a much-improved defense, has put the Detroit Lions in a position that seemed unfathomable at the end of October.
headlines.shortLinkText: Lions rout Bears 41-10 and take playoff hopes to Green Bay

events.name: Denver Broncos at Kansas City Chiefs

headlines.description: — Patrick Mahomes moved into the rarest of company Sunday, joining Tom Brady and Drew Brees as the only quarterbacks with multiple 5,000-yard passing seasons, and the Chiefs overcame another sloppy start to beat the Broncos for the 15th consecutive...
headlines.shortLinkText: Sluggish Chiefs beat Broncos for 15th straight time, 27-24

events.name: Miami Dolphins at New England Patriots

headlines.description: — His team clutching a two-point lead, the ball on the 1-yard line and the season in the balance, Mac Jones decided to improvise.
headlines.shortLinkText: Dugger INT return helps lift Pats over fading Dolphins 23-21

events.name: Indianapolis Colts at New York Giants

headlines.description: — The Gatorade bath for the head coach returned. Fans counted down the final seconds and there were fireworks above MetLife Stadium at the final tick. Tears were shed in the locker room.
headlines.shortLinkText: Giants rout Colts, reach playoffs for 1st time since 2016

events.name: New Orleans Saints at Philadelphia Eagles

headlines.description: — Jalen Hurts held a clipboard, wore a headset and paced the sideline with the same the look of consternation on his face as furious Philly fans in the stands as their stress tightened in another Eagles loss without the franchise quarterback.
headlines.shortLinkText: Saints win 20-10 in Philly, deny Eagles top spot in NFC

events.name: Carolina Panthers at Tampa Bay Buccaneers

headlines.description: — Tom Brady and the Tampa Bay Buccaneers are headed back to the playoffs, confident they're still capable of making this a special season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brady, Bucs rally to beat Panthers 30-24, clinch NFC South

headlines.description: — Fans booed Carson Wentz and chanted for Taylor Heinicke to no avail while it was becoming abundantly clear Ron Rivera's quarterback change...
headlines.shortLinkText: Wentz, Commanders lose to Browns, eliminated from contention

headlines.description: — With so much on the line next week, it would have been easy for the Jacksonville Jaguars to overlook the lowly Houston Texans on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Jags rout Texans 31-3 to end 9-game skid vs. Houston

events.name: San Francisco 49ers at Las Vegas Raiders

headlines.description: — San Francisco coach Kyle Shanahan said his team has been in playoff mode for several weeks as it fights for a top seed in the NFC, and Sunday had a postseason atmosphere.
headlines.shortLinkText: 49ers edge Raiders 37-34 in OT for 9th consecutive win

events.name: New York Jets at Seattle Seahawks

headlines.description: — Geno Smith finished off a very personal sweep. And by him doing so, a season that started with low expectations for the Seattle Seahawks will reach Week 18 with the playoffs as a real possibility.
headlines.shortLinkText: Seahawks keep playoff hopes alive with win, eliminate Jets

events.name: Minnesota Vikings at Green Bay Packers

headlines.description: — Aaron Rodgers and the Packers suddenly are just a win away from an improbable playoff berth.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rodgers, Packers rout Vikings 41-17, control playoff fate

events.name: Los Angeles Rams at Los Angeles Chargers

headlines.description: — On a day when Austin Ekeler accomplished a receiving milestone, it was his running that paved the way for the Los Angeles Chargers to keep rolling toward the postseason.
headlines.shortLinkText: Ekeler has 2 TDs, reaches 100 catches as Chargers rout Rams

headlines.description: — A lot still has to go right for the Pittsburgh Steelers to make the playoffs.
headlines.shortLinkText: Steelers still alive after last-minute 16-13 win over Ravens
